 {"id":"bd-313","title":"Expand benchmark coverage beyond buffer module","description":"Currently only benches/buffer.rs exists. Add benchmarks for: renderer diff detection, color blending operations, text buffer operations, unicode width calculations.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T20:28:46.184813379Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T22:37:44.657047735Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T22:37:44.656970329Z","close_reason":"Comprehensive benchmarks added for color blending (creation, blending, conversion, interpolation), text buffer (TextBuffer, EditBuffer, cursor movement, undo/redo), renderer diff detection (identical, single change, row change, scattered, full diff), and unicode width calculations (ASCII, CJK, emoji, grapheme operations). All benchmarks compile and run successfully.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-31gz","title":"demo_showcase docs: README section (how to run + flags + keybinds)","description":"# demo_showcase Docs — README Section (How to Run + Flags + Keybinds)

## Purpose

The flagship demo should be discoverable. Add a README section that explains:

- how to run the demo
- what it demonstrates
- essential keybinds
- tour mode and headless smoke mode
- recommended terminals for best visuals

## Content Requirements

Add a `Demo Showcase` section to `README.md`:

Run:
- cargo run --bin demo_showcase

Tour (unattended):
- cargo run --bin demo_showcase -- --tour --exit-after-tour

Headless smoke:
- cargo run --bin demo_showcase -- --headless-smoke

Headless JSON dump (for tests/debugging):
- cargo run --bin demo_showcase -- --headless-smoke --headless-dump-json

Degradation matrix (example manual run):
- cargo run --bin demo_showcase -- --headless-smoke --headless-dump-json --headless-size 50x15 --cap-preset minimal

Document keybinds (or point to the in-app help overlay) and call out terminal recommendations (kitty/alacritty/wezterm/ghostty) for best results.

## Flag Summary (Must Match bd-2iv)

Include a small, explicit list of supported flags (grouped by category):

Interactive:
- --fps <N>
- --tour
- --exit-after-tour
- --max-frames <N>

Headless/testing:
- --headless-smoke
- --headless-dump-json
- --headless-check <name>
- --headless-size <WxH>

Terminal behavior:
- --no-mouse
- --no-alt-screen
- --no-cap-queries

Capability override:
- --cap-preset <name>

Advanced:
- --threaded
- --seed <N>

## Acceptance Notes

- README makes it obvious the demo exists and how to run it.
- Flags documented match the actual CLI (bd-2iv).","acceptance_criteria":"Done when:
- `README.md` contains a clear `Demo Showcase` section with run commands (interactive, tour, headless).
- The documented flag list matches the implemented CLI (`bd-2iv`) including headless size + capability preset controls.
- The README points users to the in-app help overlay for the authoritative keybind list.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:29:37.288336317Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T03:59:51.498373446Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T03:59:51.498306461Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","docs"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31gz","depends_on_id":"bd-1gy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:38.026379403Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-31gz","depends_on_id":"bd-2iv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:36.008108527Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-31gz","depends_on_id":"bd-383","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:29:37.300717928Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-31gz","depends_on_id":"bd-3l0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:37.034595590Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-31s2","title":"Implement scroll_up_in_region(lines) - Scroll up","description":"# Task: Implement scroll_up_in_region()

## Function Signature (Rust)
```rust
pub fn scroll_up_in_region(&mut self, lines: u32)
```

## Purpose
Scrolls the content within the scroll region up by N lines. New blank lines appear at bottom.

## ANSI Sequence
ESC [ <n> S (SU - Scroll Up)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Outputs correct sequence
- [ ] Handles lines=0

## Testing
- Scroll various amounts","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:33:34.313909553Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T15:33:34.324762492Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31s2","depends_on_id":"bd-2ij1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:33:34.324723890Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-31xg","title":"Implement get_stats() - Pool statistics","description":"# Task: Implement get_stats()

## Function Signature (Rust)
```rust
pub fn get_stats(&self) -> PoolStats
```

## PoolStats Type
```rust
#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, Default)]
pub struct PoolStats {
    pub total_slots: usize,       // Total allocated slots
    pub active_count: usize,      // Currently in-use slots
    pub freed_count: usize,       // Freed (reusable) slots
    pub peak_usage: usize,        // Maximum active at once
    pub total_allocations: u64,   // Lifetime allocation count
    pub total_frees: u64,         // Lifetime free count
}
```

## Purpose
Returns comprehensive statistics about the grapheme pool. Used for debugging, monitoring, and optimization decisions.

## Implementation Notes
- Track stats during alloc/free operations
- Peak usage updated when active_count increases
- Counters should be O(1) maintenance

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] PoolStats struct defined
- [ ] All fields populated correctly
- [ ] O(1) to retrieve

## Testing
- Empty pool stats
- After allocations
- After frees
- Peak tracking","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:35:39.886514802Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T22:25:08.316064140Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31xg","depends_on_id":"bd-g3az","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:35:39.896617322Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-32u8","title":"Implement clear_segments() - Remove all segments","description":"# Task: Implement clear_segments()

## Function Signature (Rust)
```rust
pub fn clear_segments(&mut self)
```

## Purpose
Removes all segments from the buffer. Used when replacing highlighting or resetting state.

## Implementation Notes
- Clear segment storage
- Mark entire buffer dirty
- Consider optimization: only dirty if had segments

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All segments removed
- [ ] Buffer marked dirty
- [ ] Can add segments after clear

## Testing
- Clear populated buffer
- Clear empty buffer","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:28:15.910518562Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T15:28:15.926632550Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-32u8","depends_on_id":"bd-21ap","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:28:15.926598266Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-33pm","title":"Implement get_frame_stats() - Frame statistics","description":"# Task: Implement get_frame_stats()

## Function Signature (Rust)
```rust
pub fn get_frame_stats(&self) -> FrameStats
```

## Purpose
Returns statistics for the most recent rendered frame. Useful for performance monitoring, debugging, and adaptive rendering decisions.

## Return Type
```rust
#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, Default)]
pub struct FrameStats {
    pub render_time_us: u64,      // Microseconds to render
    pub cells_total: u32,         // Total cells in buffer
    pub cells_changed: u32,       // Cells that changed from previous frame
    pub cells_rendered: u32,      // Cells actually rendered (after diff)
    pub bytes_written: usize,     // ANSI bytes written to output
    pub frame_number: u64,        // Monotonic frame counter
}
```

## Implementation Notes
1. Track stats during present_diff/present_force
2. Store in Renderer struct
3. Measure time with std::time::Instant
4. Count cells during diff detection

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] FrameStats struct defined
- [ ] Timing measurement implemented
- [ ] Cell counts accurate
- [ ] Byte count tracked
- [ ] Frame counter increments

## Testing
- Stats after initial render
- Stats after partial update
- Stats after force render
- Verify counts match actual output","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:26:08.746158478Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T16:16:28.026962087Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T16:16:28.026899410Z","close_reason":"ALREADY EXISTS: stats() returns RenderStats in src/renderer/mod.rs:217","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-33pm","depends_on_id":"bd-20k1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:26:08.755232918Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3413","title":"Implement move_cursor_relative(dx, dy) - Relative cursor move","description":"# Task: Implement move_cursor_relative()

## Function Signature (Rust)
```rust
pub fn move_cursor_relative(&mut self, dx: i32, dy: i32)
```

## Purpose
Moves cursor by relative delta. More efficient than absolute positioning when nearby.

## ANSI Sequences
- Up: ESC [ n A
- Down: ESC [ n B
- Right: ESC [ n C
- Left: ESC [ n D

## Implementation Notes
- Choose appropriate sequence based on sign
- Combine into single output
- Skip if delta is 0

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Correct sequence for each direction
- [ ] Handles zero delta
- [ ] Efficient for small moves

## Testing
- Move in each direction
- Mixed directions
- Zero delta","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:32:33.347794334Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T15:32:33.361149816Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3413","depends_on_id":"bd-5yy0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:32:33.361114420Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
